cards.prompt: The tree is built bottom-up: two Expression.Constant leaves feed Expression.Add, capped by Expression.Lambda<Func<int>>; Compile()() compiles to a delegate and invokes it, so 1+2 prints 3
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; var one = Expression.Constant(1, typeof(int)); var two = Expression.Constant(2, typeof(int)); var addition = Expression.Add(one, two); var lambda = Expression.Lambda<Func<int>>(addition); Console.WriteLine(lambda.Compile()());

cards.prompt: The same ParameterExpression x is used in the body Add(x, Constant(1)) and in the Lambda parameter list; ToString() renders the tree as x => (x + 1) and Compile()(41) yields 42, so the output is x => (x + 1) 42
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; var x = Expression.Parameter(typeof(int),"x"); var body = Expression.Add(x, Expression.Constant(1)); var lambda = Expression.Lambda<Func<int,int>>(body, x); var f = lambda.Compile(); Console.WriteLine($"{lambda} {f(41)}");

cards.prompt: The manually built num => num < 5 tree has a BinaryExpression body with NodeType=LessThan, and Compile()(3) evaluates 3<5 to True, so the output is LessThan True
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; var numParam = Expression.Parameter(typeof(int), "num"); var five = Expression.Constant(5, typeof(int)); var numLessThanFive = Expression.LessThan(numParam, five); var lambda1 = Expression.Lambda<Func<int,bool>>(numLessThanFive, new[]{numParam}); Console.WriteLine($"{lambda1.Body.NodeType} {lambda1.Compile()(3)}");
